title: Fixing Type Inference for Custom Objects

statement.markdown: Given an array of objects with properties `name` and `age`, implement a function to return a new array containing only the objects with `age` greater than 18. However, the TypeScript type checker is unable to infer the type of the returned array correctly.
statement.inputFormat: An array of objects with `name` and `age` properties.
statement.outputFormat: A new array containing only the objects with `age` greater than 18.
statement.notes: Note that the type checker should be able to infer the type of the returned array automatically.

constraints.description: The number of objects in the input array.

examples.input: [{ name: 'John', age: 20 }, { name: 'Jane', age: 15 }, { name: 'Bob', age: 22 }]
examples.explanation: The function should return a new array containing only the objects with `age` greater than 18.

hints.text: Use the `filter` method to create a new array containing only the objects with `age` greater than 18.

editorial.title: Fixing Type Inference for Custom Objects
editorial.intuition: The type checker is unable to infer the type of the returned array because it does not have enough information about the properties of the objects in the array.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible solution is to use a type assertion, but this is not necessary and can make the code less maintainable.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution is to use the `filter` method, which creates a new array containing only the objects that satisfy the given condition.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the `filter` method and add a type annotation for the returned array, like this: `const result = array.filter(obj => obj.age > 18);`. This tells the type checker that the returned array contains objects with the same properties as the objects in the input array.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The `filter` method returns a new array containing only the objects that satisfy the given condition, which means that the returned array contains only objects with `age` greater than 18.
editorial.dryRun: Here's an example of how the code would run with the `filter` method: `const array = [{ name: 'John', age: 20 }, { name: 'Jane', age: 15 }, { name: 'Bob', age: 22 }]; const result = array.filter(obj => obj.age > 18); console.log(result);`
commonMistakes: Not adding a type annotation for the returned array

testCases.input: [{ name: 'John', age: 20 }, { name: 'Jane', age: 15 }, { name: 'Bob', age: 22 }]
[{ name: 'Alice', age: 25 }, { name: 'Bob', age: 30 }, { name: 'Charlie', age: 35 }]
testCases.output: [{ name: 'Alice', age: 25 }, { name: 'Bob', age: 30 }, { name: 'Charlie', age: 35 }]
